openQuestions.question: Q1 — Approve the Save-draft/Publish-to-Webflow spine (new server capability, sn-page-rewriter-1) or ship export-only parity first? Parity Ledger's 'Apply back to CMS' func claim is wrong and needs correcting either way.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Export-only parity v1; hide Save draft/Publish behind a flag; correct the ledger row.

openQuestions.question: Q2 — Confirm .docx/.pdf stay in the export menu and define what 'push to a new draft' targets.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep all four HEAD export modes + add Copy-as-HTML; defer push-to-draft until Q1 resolves its destination.

openQuestions.question: Q3 — Carry a 'Rewrite this page' deep-link (initialPageUrl) into the new IA (e.g. from SEO Editor Research mode / audit rows) or formally retire the contract?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Carry: wire the deep-link from SEO Editor Research mode (D3 home of Page Intelligence) — cheap and makes the dead contract live.

openQuestions.question: Q4 — Is losing document edits on navigation acceptable for v1 (status quo), or is the localStorage draft quick-win required?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Status quo for v1 (avoids over-promising 'Draft saved'); revisit with Q1.

openQuestions.question: Q5 — Confirm homes for the 8 at_risk rows (#6,20,21,28,32,34,36,37), especially audit issue chips (#28) and edit-before-apply (#20) which have no prototype slot.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Carry all 8 (additive-parity floor): chips strip above the document, editable rewrite bubble kept, clear-format in fixed toolbar, live-page link on header URL, docx/pdf in export menu; #6 per Q3.

openQuestions.question: Prototype uses per-block contenteditable; HEAD uses one contenteditable body. Which block model does the rebuild adopt?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep HEAD single-body model (T1 carry-over-then-reskin — full parity incl. #21 apply targeting); per-block is C3-style UX polish.

kitNewFeatures.rationale: No persistence exists; needs a storage decision (Q1/Q4) and a table+lifecycle — not a parity rebuild item.
Hard write-target problem (static DOM vs CMS fields); zero write path at HEAD — new product capability, owner-gated (Q1).

kitNewFeatures.rationale: Data already exists (page_keywords); one small server field (sn-page-rewriter-3) — high trust-signal value for low cost.

lensesOrTabs: Single view, no lenses: two-pane workspace (chat pane | document pane)
primitives: Toolbar (+ToolbarSpacer) — header bar and fixed formatting toolbar
SearchField — page combobox base (keyboard/ARIA re-spec needed)
Note: chat bubbles + contentEditable document are bespoke carried machinery (T1), not F3 primitives
composition.statesPlan: Empty: no-page CTA + empty-sitemap paste-URL fallback; Loading: contextual spinner ('Loading page...'); Error: fetch-failure with HTTP status detail + retry; Locked: needsSite nav lock per four-states rule.
composition.mountNote: Flag-gated on ui-rebuild-shell inside F4 RebuiltAppChrome; Page 'rewrite' under Optimization NavGroup; preserve per-workspace remount key and focus-mode shell contract.

crossSurfaceDeps: F4 AppShell: focus mode requires the rebuilt shell to support rail collapse + Esc exit + nav auto-reset (AppShell frozen post-F3 — needs a shell hook, not a fork)
SEO Audit / SEO Editor (D3): back-button target and the Q3 'Rewrite this page' deep-link origin live on those surfaces
seo-editor write-target contract (docs/rules/seo-editor-write-targets.md) if Q1 approves publish — shared write-target resolution
D5 icons: WandSparkles (lucide) nav icon migrates to an ICON_NAMES key
Parity Ledger correction: 'Apply back to CMS' func row overstates HEAD (program-level doc fix)

verdicts.evidence: server/routes/rewrite-chat.ts has only 3 routes (GET /pages:158, POST /load-page:175, POST chat:217) — all reads/AI-generation, zero write-back, no draft/publish. server/routes/webflow-seo-rewrite.ts:3-6 header declares '@writes none' (returns rewrite variations only). SEO Editor apply (server/routes/webflow-seo-apply.ts:85-88) writes ONLY seo.title/seo.description via updatePageSeo — meta fields, not full-page body. No rewrite-drafts table: `rg 'CREATE TABLE' migrations` finds nothing for rewritten page content (briefing_drafts @077 is a different surface — briefs). No rewrite publish/draft/save endpoint anywhere.
verdicts.correction: Claim stands as written. existsToday=no confirmed; effort L defensible (new migration + draft/publish lifecycle + Webflow static-DOM/CMS write-target resolution + activity/broadcast). Correctly gated on Q1.

verdicts.evidence: The rewrite-playbook patterns array (intelligence.contentPipeline.rewritePlaybook.patterns, page-assist-context-builder.ts:128,152-155) is prompt-injection-only — no dedicated UI read endpoint. The GET /api/outcomes/:workspaceId/playbooks (server/routes/outcomes.ts:582) is a DIFFERENT playbook (outcome/action patterns from outcome-playbooks.ts), NOT the rewrite playbook. HOWEVER: the RAW rewrite-playbook string IS ALREADY serialized to the admin UI (server/serializers/admin-workspace-view.ts:41 `rewritePlaybook: ws.rewritePlaybook`, served via server/routes/workspaces.ts:67 toAdminWorkspaceView). The slice 'patterns' array is a trivial rawPlaybook.split('\n') (content-pipeline-slice.ts:258-264), so the UI can already derive chips from the raw string on the workspace view without any new endpoint.
verdicts.correction: Direction right (no dedicated patterns endpoint exists; buildPageAssistContext is prompt-only), but the 'full version' may be UNNECESZARY: the raw playbook string is already delivered to the admin UI via the workspace view serializer, and patterns = string.split('\n'). Before building a new GET endpoint, confirm the rewriter UI can't just consume the existing workspace-view rewritePlaybook field and split client-side — that could reduce this from S to zero net-new backend work. Effort S is otherwise correct if a dedicated endpoint is still wanted.

verdicts.evidence: rewrite-chat.ts load-page response (line 203) has no keyword field. listPageKeywords(workspaceId) exists (server/page-keywords.ts:505) returning PageKeywordMap[]; PageKeywordMap (shared/types/workspace.ts:76) carries primaryKeyword keyed by pagePath (rowToLiteModel:544 sets primaryKeyword: row.primary_keyword). It is a pure read (SELECT + rowToModel, no writes/snapshot side-effects). The load-page handler already derives `slug` at line 193, so targetKeyword resolves via a slug-match against listPageKeywords. No migration, no mapper, no serialization lockstep — the column already exists.
verdicts.correction: Claim stands; effort S correct. Minor efficiency note (not a refutation): listPageKeywords assembles per-page score histories for ALL pages (groupScoreHistory) which is heavier than needed for a single-slug lookup on a hot load-page call — prefer listPageKeywordsLite (page-keywords.ts:561, skips history) or a slug-scoped read to keep the read cheap. Does not change effort/existsToday.
